titleStmt.title: SII 18.16: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 16. (B.K. No. 31 of 1932-33.) DĒVĪHOSŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Hero-stone in the temple of Banaśaṅkari.

summary.p: This inscription referring itself to the reign of Akālavarsha (apparently Kṛishṇa II), is engraved in cursive characters, and is dated in Śaka 829, Prabhava, corresponding to A.D. 907-08. No further details of the date are given. It states that when Rājāti (Rājāditya) was governing Banavāsi-nāḍu, Saruvvayya-seṭṭi son of Permmāḍi Nāyiga died in a fight. It also states that his brother Chika-seṭṭi set up the stone.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Ōṁ svasti śrī śakakālātītasaṁvvatsara Eṇṭunūṟairppa Oṁbhattaneya prabhavaṁ varttise Akālavarṣa
pṛthivīrājyaṁgeye rājāti banavāsināḍāḷe nibhayada baḷegāṟa permmā
ḍi nāyigana maga saruvvayyaseṭṭi kuḷare kāḷegadoḷe kādi kondu toṟadu
sattu svarggālayakkēridōn Avana tamma cikaseṭṭi kalla nirisidōn
sāsirbaru Ūrānusādhakanu pakkale
Ire ghayyasāsa baredo
ghanasigana besanu
Digital edition of SII 18.16 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
